_meta.note: descriptive record only, never causal attribution; all sources open

today_snapshot.note: build-time forecast; the browser re-fetches live on every visit

perception_gap.source: Andre, Boneva, Chopra & Falk 2024, Nature Climate Change; per-country via Our World in Data (CC-BY)
perception_gap.construct: share saying they would contribute 1% of household income, every month; vs average guess
perception_gap._verify: figures + exact item wording re-verified against the paper at build

how_we_know.what_is_era5: ERA5 is the ECMWF's hour-by-hour reconstruction of global weather since 1940, built by feeding every available measurement (stations, ships, satellites) into a physics model on a ~30 km grid.
how_we_know.bias_note: measured in the spike, Apr–Jun 2026; remeasured periodically
how_we_know.station_validation: declared next step: cross-check vs a licence-clean nearby station series

budget.rate_src: Global Carbon Budget 2025 (Global Carbon Project) — total CO2 incl. land use; fossil alone 38.1

estimates.label: IGCC 2025 — Forster et al., ESSD
estimates.src: Indicators of Global Climate Change 2025 — Forster et al., ESSD

human_induced.src: IGCC 2025 (Forster et al.) — human-induced warming vs 1850-1900

scc.src: US EPA, Dec 2023 — 2% discount rate; assumption-dependent range

notes: Budgets are re-estimated yearly and have been revised downward; we show both current estimates, never an average.
1.5 °C is a dial of rising risk, not a cliff: worse at 1.6 than 1.5, worse again at 1.7.
Years = remaining budget / rate, held from today - arithmetic, not a pathway model.
Each budget is published against its own start date; we subtract emissions elapsed since that date at the current rate to bring both to one day. The subtraction is shown in the table.
budget._verify: figures, anchors and the held-rate assumption re-verified against the named releases annually

sky.src: Yale Bright Star Catalogue via d3-celestial (Olaf Frohn, BSD-3-Clause); alt/az computed in-browser for Delhi (Meeus / Schlyter low-precision algorithms)
